decision: Keep analytics DIP changes limited to the seven scoped analytics files already modified in the worktree
reason: The task metadata defines the execution scope precisely, and the existing changes already introduce interface-first seams in models, repository/service wiring, and scoped tests without requiring broader analytics refactors.
impact: Avoided touching non-scoped analytics implementation files while preserving the dependency inversion intent of the queued task.

decision: Record  as environment-blocked rather than code-blocked
reason: Targeted analytics tests, boundary checks, and gofmt passed, but  exhausted the available disk/tmp space while compiling dependencies, so the failure does not identify a package-specific source issue.
impact: Summary keeps verification accurate and calls out the remaining infrastructure follow-up instead of inventing an analytics defect.
